ThinkPHP Pagination: Built-in Pages and a Custom Bootstrap Style

Next up I want to talk about a feature pretty much every website needs at some point: pagination. ThinkPHP actually gives you pagination out of the box, so the question is just how to use it. That's what this post is about.
ThinkPHP comes with pagination built in. It's a pretty basic style, but all you really need to do is call
and pair it with some HTML and you're done. Let me just show you with code. For the example I'm going to reuse the
and modify it. But before that, here's what the data looks like right now.
I've added the data up to 10 records
Say I only want to output 5 records per page and then flip to the next page. I'll just copy
language-php shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Under normal conditions you should see only 5 records showing up.
language-html shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
— ThinkPHP automatically swaps that out for the pagination control.
You might also run into another way of writing it,
. The result is exactly the same, so just pick whichever one you like.
Honestly, customizing the pagination style is a bit of a hassle, but the default look is pretty plain, so let's fix it up.

This post mainly covered how to create your own file inside the ThinkPHP core. But generally speaking, anything you modify yourself or any third-party extension is better off living in
. The main reason is you don't want to touch the core code, because the core might need updating down the line and that would overwrite your file. That's exactly why I also showed the
approach — and in practice, that's the one I'd lean on.

Pagination is one of those features pretty much every site needs, and the good news is ThinkPHP ships with it out of the box. In this post we'll call paginate(), wire it up with some HTML, and get a working pager. Then we'll go a step further and override the default Bootstrap style with our own. Finally, I'll show you the cleaner way to load that custom paginator from the extend folder instead of hacking the core.
